analysis.overall_assessment: The image shows a single, unhealthy tea leaf with brown spots on its surface.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Implement integrated pest management (IPM) strategies to reduce herbicide use
Rotate crops and diversify plant species in tea fields to minimize chemical exposure
Invest in organic or natural alternatives for weed control

immediate_actions: Sort out the unhealthy leaf from the healthy ones to prevent cross-contamination
Inspect other leaves in the batch for similar symptoms and remove any affected ones
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Carefully sort through each tea leaf, removing those with visible brown spots or burnt edges
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Process only healthy leaves to avoid contamination of the final product
quality_preservation: Ensure proper sanitation and cleanliness in processing equipment to prevent cross-contamination

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: The healthy parts of the tea leaves can still be used for making tea
alternative_uses: Use burnt or damaged leaves in baking, cooking, or as a natural fertilizer
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Compost unhealthy leaves separately from healthy ones to prevent contamination
prevention_measures: Implement regular monitoring and inspection of tea plants for early detection of pollution symptoms

cost_saving_opportunities: Invest in more sustainable and environmentally friendly farming practices
value_recovery_methods: Explore alternative uses for burnt or damaged leaves to minimize waste

priority_actions: Rotate crops and diversify plant species in tea fields
Invest in organic or natural alternatives for weed control
